overview.short: Rangayyanadurga Four–horned antelope Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in Karnataka India, aimed for the protection of Four-horned antelopes (Tetracerus quadricornis).
overview.description: Rangayyanadurga Four–horned antelope Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in Karnataka India, aimed for the protection of Four-horned antelopes (Tetracerus quadricornis). The four-horned antelope is classified as Vulnerable species by the International Union for the Conservation of Nature and Natural Resources (IUCN). Established in 2011, it is the only four-horned antelope sanctuary in Karnataka. In 2017, Indian government tagged an area of 0.95 to 4.5 km from the boundary of the sanctuary as an eco-sensitive zone (ESZ).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Rangayyanadurga Four–horned antelope Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Rangayyanadurga Four–horned antelope Wildlife Sanctuary is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Rangayyanadurga Four–horned antelope Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Bellary Airport (108 km) and the nearest railway station is Hubballi Junction (139 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Rangayyanadurga Four–horned antelope Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Rangayyanadurga Four–horned antelope Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Rangayyanadurga Four–horned antelope Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Rangayyanadurga Four–horned antelope Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.
